---
title: "master_thesis_site_deepdive"
author: "Savannah K. Miller"
date: "2026-06-22"
output: html_document
---

```{r, working directory and packages}



# data import and database management 
# reading files, reshaping data, and efficient data handling 

#######################################################

library(readxl) # import excel spreadsheets
library(data.table) #quick manipulation of large datasets
library(stringr) # text and string processing 
library(lubridate) # data and time handling 
library(writexl) # exports editted databases into excel files

#######################################################

# General data science workflow 
# Collection of packages for data manipulation and plotting

#######################################################

library(tidyverse)

#######################################################

#Statistical analyses and mixed models 
# Hypothesis testing, summaries, and model fitting 

#######################################################

library(gt) # displaying formatted tables for statistics 
library(rstatix) # statistical tests and assumption checking
library(lmerTest) # p-values and tests for lme4 models 
library(lme4) # linear and generalized mixed-effects models
library(ggeffects) # predicted values and marginal effects 

#######################################################

# Data visualization 
# Plot creation, figure assembly, and publication formatting 

#######################################################

library(patchwork) #combine ggplots into multi-panel figures
library(cowplot) # figure arrangement and plot annotations
library(rcartocolor) # color palettes
library(ggh4x) # advanced ggplot extensions
library(scales) # axis labels, formatting, and scaling 
library(ggridges)

#######################################################
# Spatial data and mapping 
# GIS data handling and map visualization
#######################################################

library(sf) #spatial vector data
library(ggmap) #basemaps and geographic visualization
library(ggspatial) #spatial annotations and map elements
library(leaflet) #interactive web maps

#######################################################

#Tables and report outputs 
# Interactive tables and report presentation 

#######################################################

library(reactable) #interactive tables for reports

#######################################################
# R markdown and document rendering 
# PDF generation and LaTeX support 
#######################################################

library(tinytex) #LaTeX engine for PDF knitting
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r, load the datasheet}

master_thesis = readr::write_csv(sed_thesis, "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_thesis.csv")

str(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field)
head(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field, 10)
sum(is.na(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field))

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r, load the datasheet}

master_thesis <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_thesis.csv")


# Reassign factors to appropriate variables 

str(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field)
head(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field, 10)
sum(is.na(master_thesis$Date.placed.in.field))


```



```{r, site B}

# Filter to just San Pablo with elev/flux within it 

s_elev <- elev_z_flux %>%
  filter(Site == "SanPablo")


# Summarize by Transect and Date.removed.from.field instead of by Site
s_summary <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(
    Tran,
    Date.removed.from.field
  ) %>%
summarise(
  mean_flux = mean(Flux[Flux >= 0], na.rm = TRUE),
  se_flux = sd(Flux[Flux >= 0], na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(Flux >= 0, na.rm = TRUE)),
  .groups = "drop"
)

ggplot(
  s_summary,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = Tran,
    group = Tran
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks = unique(s_summary$Date.removed.from.field),
    date_labels = "%b %d",
    guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2)
  ) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  ) +  
  labs(
    title = "San Pablo",
    x = "Date Removed from Field",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    caption = "Error bars represent ± 1 standard error of the mean"
  )


readr::write_csv(s_elev, "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_thesis_s.csv")
```


```{r, for s data}

# Standard caption for plots containing error bars
se_caption <- paste(
  "Points represent transect-level mean flux.",
  "Error bars represent +/- 1 standard error calculated from observations within each transect."
)

# Common theme
flux_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      size = 10,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 8)
    ),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# basic

basic_flux_s <- ggplot(
  s_elev,
  aes(x = Flux)
) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 30,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Tran) +
  flux_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Flux Distribution by Transect at San Pablo",
    x = expression(Flux~(g/m^2)),
    y = "Count"
  )

basic_flux_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\basic_flux_s.png"),
  plot = basic_flux_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# flux through time 

removal_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Date.removed.from.field) %>%
  summarise(
    n_flux = sum(!is.na(Flux)),
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = if_else(
      n_flux > 1,
      sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n_flux),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

meanflux_time_s <- ggplot(
  removal_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = factor(Tran)
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 2,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 3),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    position = position_dodge(width = 3)
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks = sort(unique(removal_summary_s$Date.removed.from.field)),
    date_labels = "%b %d\n%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.03, 0.03))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 30)) +
  flux_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Flux Through Time by Transect at San Pablo",
    subtitle = "Negative flux values set to zero",
    x = "Date removed from field",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Transect",
    caption = se_caption
  )

meanflux_time_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\meanflux_time_s.png"),
  plot = meanflux_time_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# flux by Phen_Period 

hydro_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Phen_Period) %>%
  summarise(
    n_flux = sum(!is.na(Flux)),
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = if_else(
      n_flux > 1,
      sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n_flux),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

meanflux_hydro_s <- ggplot(
  hydro_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Phen_Period,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = factor(Tran)
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.65)
  ) +
  flux_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 30,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Flux by Hydrologic Period and Transect at San Pablo",
    x = "Hydrologic Period",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Transect",
    caption = se_caption
  )

meanflux_hydro_s

meanflux_time_s2 <- ggplot(
  removal_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = mean_flux
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 2,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ Tran) +
  scale_x_date(
    breaks = sort(unique(removal_summary_s$Date.removed.from.field)),
    date_labels = "%b %d\n%Y",
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.03, 0.03))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 30)) +
  flux_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      size = 8
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Flux Through Time by Transect at San Pablo",
    subtitle = "Negative flux values set to zero",
    x = "Date removed from field",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    caption = se_caption
  )

meanflux_time_s2

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\meanflux_time_s2.png"),
  plot = meanflux_time_s2,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)



# flux by distance 

dist_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Dist) %>%
  summarise(
    n_flux = sum(!is.na(Flux)),
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = if_else(
      n_flux > 1,
      sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n_flux),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

meanflux_dist_s <- ggplot(
  dist_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Dist,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = factor(Tran)
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 1,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1.5),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1.5)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(dist_summary$Dist_numeric))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 9.5)) +
  flux_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Flux by Distance and Transect at San Pablo",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Transect",
    caption = se_caption
  )

meanflux_dist_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\meanflux_dist_s.png"),
  plot = meanflux_dist_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# flux by z_star


z_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, z_star) %>%
  summarise(
    n_flux = sum(!is.na(Flux)),
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = if_else(
      n_flux > 1,
      sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n_flux),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

meanflux_z_s <- ggplot(
  z_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = mean_flux,
    y = z_star,
    color = Tran
  )
) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(
      xmin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      xmax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    height = 0.01,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    n.breaks = 8
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = expression("Mean Flux by "*z^"*"*" and Transect at San Pablo"),
    x = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    y = expression(z^"*"),
    color = "Transect",
    caption = paste(
      "Points represent mean flux within each transect and z* value.",
      "Horizontal error bars represent +/- 1 standard error."
    )
  )

meanflux_z_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\meanflux_z_s.png"),
  plot = meanflux_z_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# raw flux by z star

flux_z_s = ggplot(s_elev,
       aes(x = z_star,
           y = Flux,
           color = Tran)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

flux_z_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_z_s.png"),
  plot = flux_z_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# raw flux with z as a GAM ( )

flux_z_s_gam = ggplot(s_elev,
       aes(x = z_star,
           y = Flux)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Tran),
             size = 3,
             alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "gam",
    formula = y ~ s(x),
    color = "black"
  )

flux_z_s_gam

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_z_s_gam.png"),
  plot = flux_z_s_gam,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


# flux by distance and hydro

dist_hydro_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Dist, Phen_Period) %>%
  summarise(
    n_flux = sum(!is.na(Flux)),
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = if_else(
      n_flux > 1,
      sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n_flux),
      NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

flux_dist_hydro_s <- ggplot(
  dist_hydro_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Dist,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = factor(Tran)
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 1,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1.5),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    size = 3,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1.5)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Phen_Period) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(dist_hydro_summary$Dist_numeric))
  ) +
  flux_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Flux by Distance, Hydrologic Period, and Transect",
    subtitle = "San Pablo",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Transect",
    caption = se_caption
  )

flux_dist_hydro_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_dist_hydro_s.png"),
  plot = flux_dist_hydro_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# flux through time by distance 

time_dist_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Dist, Date.removed.from.field) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


flux_dist_gradient_s <- ggplot(
  time_dist_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Dist,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = factor(Date.removed.from.field)
  )
) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 1.2,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    position = position_dodge(width = 2),
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = factor(Date.removed.from.field)),
    size = 3.5,
    shape = 21,
    color = "black",
    stroke = 0.4,
    position = position_dodge(width = 2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Tran) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(
    option = "D",
    end = 0.85
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(
    option = "D",
    end = 0.85
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(time_dist_summary_s$Dist)), 
    guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 2)
    )  +
  scale_y_continuous(n.breaks = 10) +
  flux_theme +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.width = unit(1.1, "cm")
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(nrow = 2),
    fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Flux at Discrete Marsh-Edge Sampling Points",
    subtitle = "San Pablo; seven sampling distances across 50 m",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Collection date",
    fill = "Collection date",
    caption = se_caption
  )

flux_dist_gradient_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_dist_gradient_s.png"),
  plot = flux_dist_gradient_s,
  width = 14,
  height = 9,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# heat map and distance
flux_heatmap_s = ggplot(
  time_dist_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = Dist,
    fill = mean_flux
  )
) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(mean_flux, 1)),
    color = "white",
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", name = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2))) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.key.height = unit(1.5, "cm")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Flux Heatmap: Study Phase x Distance at", "San Pablo"),
    x = "Date removed from Field",
    y = "Distance from Marsh Edge"
  )
flux_heatmap_s

ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_heatmap_s.png"),
  plot = flux_heatmap_s,
  width = 12,
  height = 9,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# flux through time by transect 

tran_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Date.removed.from.field) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

flux_tran_time_s = ggplot(
  tran_summary,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = Tran,
    group = Tran
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + 
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,30)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Flux Through Time by Transect at", "San Pablo"),
    x = "Date removed from field",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^2)),
    color = "Transect"
  )
flux_tran_time_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_tran_time_s.png"),
  plot = flux_tran_time_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


# raw flux and distance 

rawflux_dist_s = ggplot(
  s_elev,
  aes(
    x = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)),
    y = Flux
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,120)) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Raw Flux vs Distance at", "San Pablo"),
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
  )
rawflux_dist_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\rawflux_dist_s.png"),
  plot = rawflux_dist_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# elev and flux

flux_elev_s = ggplot(s_elev,
   aes(
     x = Elevation_m,
     y = Flux
   )
 ) +
   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
   labs(
     title = paste("Flux vs Elevation at", "San Pablo"),
     x = "Elevation_m",
     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
   )

flux_elev_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_elev_s.png"),
  plot = flux_elev_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# z star and flux

flux_z_s = ggplot(
  s_elev,
   aes(
     x = z_star,
     y = Flux
   )
 ) +
   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
   labs(
     title = paste("Flux vs Elevation at", "San Pablo"),
     x = "z_star",
     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
   )

flux_z_s

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flux_z_s.png"),
  plot = flux_z_s,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# once veg is cleaned and entered 

# one_site <- one_site %>%
#   mutate(
#     Veg_Structure = Percent_Cover * Avg_Height
#   )

# ggplot(
#   one_site,
#   aes(
#     x = Percent_Cover,
#     y = Flux
#   )
# ) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Flux vs Percent Cover at", "San Pablo"),
#     x = "Percent Vegetation Cover",
#     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
#   )

# ggplot(
#   one_site,
#   aes(
#     x = Veg_Structure,
#     y = Flux
#   )
# ) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Flux vs Vegetation Structure at", "San Pablo"),
#     x = "Vegetation Structure Index (Cover x Height)",
#     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
#   )

# flux through time by transect 

tran_summary_s <- s_elev %>%
  group_by(Tran, Study.Phase) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_flux = mean(Flux, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_flux = sd(Flux, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(
  tran_summary_s,
  aes(
    x = Study.Phase,
    y = mean_flux,
    color = Tran,
    group = Tran
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_flux - se_flux,
      ymax = mean_flux + se_flux
    ),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Flux Through Time by Transect at", "San Pablo"),
    x = "Study Phase",
    y = expression(Mean~Flux~(g/m^3)),
    color = "Transect"
  )

# raw flux and distance 

s_flux_distance = ggplot(
s_elev,
  aes(
    x = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)),
    y = Flux
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Raw Flux vs Distance at", "San Pablo"),
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))
  )
s_flux_distance

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(("C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\s_flux_distance.png"),
  plot = s_flux_distance,
  width = 10,  
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# once elevation is complete 

# ggplot(
#   one_site,
#   aes(
#     x = Elevation,
#     y = Flux
#   )
# ) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Flux vs Elevation at", "San Pablo"),
#     x = "Elevation",
#     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))
#   )

# once veg is cleaned and entered 

# one_site <- one_site %>%
#   mutate(
#     Veg_Structure = Percent_Cover * Avg_Height
#   )

# ggplot(
#   one_site,
#   aes(
#     x = Percent_Cover,
#     y = Flux
#   )
# ) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Flux vs Percent Cover at", "San Pablo"),
#     x = "Percent Vegetation Cover",
#     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))
#   )

# ggplot(
#   one_site,
#   aes(
#     x = Veg_Structure,
#     y = Flux
#   )
# ) +
#   geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
#   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = paste("Flux vs Vegetation Structure at", "San Pablo"),
#     x = "Vegetation Structure Index (Cover x Height)",
#     y = expression(Flux~(g/m^3))
#   )

``` 






```{r}
#############################################################
# INITIAL LOOK: BUILD long_s AND A QUICK SANITY-CHECK PLOT
#
# This chunk must run FIRST -- it creates long_s via
# pivot_longer(), which the data-prep chunk
# (prepare_elevation_qc_fixed.R) then recodes and filters.
# The flag_colors / flagged_s / plots built here are a
# throwaway first look and get fully overwritten once the
# later chunks run.
#############################################################

master_thesis_s <- readr::write_csv(
  sed_thesis,
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_thesis_s.csv"
)

flag_cols <- c(
  "Filter_Num",
  "RB_Num",
  "Flood_Obsv",
  "Bio_Obsv",
  "Drop_Obsv",
  "OpenBag_Obsv",
  "Position_Obsv",
  "DryTime_Obsv",
  "NegValue_Num"
)

long_s <- s_elev %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(flag_cols),
    names_to = "Flag_Type",
    values_to = "Flag_Value"
  )

# Making a flag database
flagged_s <- long_s %>%
  filter(Flag_Value == 1) %>%
  mutate(Flag_Type = factor(Flag_Type, levels = flag_cols))

# Consistent color palette across all plots (superseded later
# by the custom hex palette in prepare_elevation_qc_fixed.R)
flag_colors <- setNames(
  scales::hue_pal()(length(flag_cols)),
  flag_cols
)

# Plot the flags
flag_summary_s <- flagged_s %>%
  group_by(Dist, Flag_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

flag_summary_s_time <- flagged_s %>%
  group_by(Date.removed.from.field, Flag_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

s_total_flags <- ggplot(flag_summary_s, aes(x = Dist, y = n, fill = Flag_Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = flag_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Flag Occurrences by Distance",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags

s_total_flags_time <- ggplot(flag_summary_s_time, aes(x = Date.removed.from.field, y = n, fill = Flag_Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = flag_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Flag Occurrences by Time",
    x = "Study.Phase",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags_time
```


```{r}
#############################################################
# INITIAL LOOK: BUILD long_s AND A QUICK SANITY-CHECK PLOT
#
# This chunk must run FIRST -- it creates long_s via
# pivot_longer(), which the data-prep chunk
# (prepare_elevation_qc_fixed.R) then recodes and filters.
# The flag_colors / flagged_s / plots built here are a
# throwaway first look and get fully overwritten once the
# later chunks run.
#############################################################

master_thesis_s <- readr::write_csv(
  sed_thesis,
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\data\\master_thesis_s.csv"
)

flag_cols <- c(
  "Filter_Num",
  "RB_Num",
  "Flood_Obsv",
  "Bio_Obsv",
  "Drop_Obsv",
  "OpenBag_Obsv",
  "Position_Obsv",
  "DryTime_Obsv",
  "NegValue_Num"
)

long_s <- s_elev %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(flag_cols),
    names_to = "Flag_Type",
    values_to = "Flag_Value"
  )

# Making a flag database
flagged_s <- long_s %>%
  filter(Flag_Value == 1) %>%
  mutate(Flag_Type = factor(Flag_Type, levels = flag_cols))

# Consistent color palette across all plots (superseded later
# by the custom hex palette in prepare_elevation_qc_fixed.R)
flag_colors <- setNames(
  scales::hue_pal()(length(flag_cols)),
  flag_cols
)

# Plot the flags
flag_summary_s <- flagged_s %>%
  group_by(Dist, Flag_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

flag_summary_s_time <- flagged_s %>%
  group_by(Date.removed.from.field, Flag_Type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")

s_total_flags <- ggplot(flag_summary_s, aes(x = Dist, y = n, fill = Flag_Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = flag_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Flag Occurrences by Distance",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags

s_total_flags_time <- ggplot(flag_summary_s_time, aes(x = Date.removed.from.field, y = n, fill = Flag_Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = flag_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Flag Occurrences by Time",
    x = "Study.Phase",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags_time

```



```{r, claude suggested flag visualizations}

#############################################################
# QC FLAG VISUALIZATIONS
#############################################################

# Shared theme for all QC plots
flag_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )


#############################################################
# 1. FLAG OCCURRENCES BY DISTANCE
#############################################################

s_total_flags <- ggplot(
  flag_summary_distance_s,
  aes(
    x = Dist,
    y = n,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    width = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Occurrences by Distance",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = "Number of Flag Occurrences",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_total_flags.png",
  plot = s_total_flags,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 2. FLAG OCCURRENCES THROUGH TIME
#############################################################

s_total_flags_time <- ggplot(
  flag_summary_time_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = n,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    width = 20
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%b %Y"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Occurrences Through Time",
    x = "Date Removed from Field",
    y = "Number of Flag Occurrences",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_total_flags_time

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_total_flags_time.png",
  plot = s_total_flags_time,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 3. SMALL MULTIPLES:
#    FLAG TYPE BY PHENOLOGICAL PERIOD AND TRANSECT
#############################################################

s_flags_period_transect <- ggplot(
  flag_counts_period_s,
  aes(
    x = Flag_Type,
    y = n,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  facet_grid(
    Phen_Period ~ Tran,
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      size = 7
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Frequency by Phenological Period and Transect",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of Flag Occurrences"
  )

s_flags_period_transect

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flags_period_transect.png",
  plot = s_flags_period_transect,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 4. FLAG TYPE BY SAMPLING DAY AND TRANSECT
#
# Faceting by every date can become very large.
#############################################################

s_flags_day_transect <- ggplot(
  flag_counts_day_s,
  aes(
    x = Flag_Type,
    y = n,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  facet_grid(
    Date.removed.from.field ~ Tran,
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      size = 6
    ),
    strip.text.y = element_text(
      size = 7,
      angle = 0
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Frequency by Sampling Day and Transect",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of Flag Occurrences"
  )

s_flags_day_transect

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flags_day_transect.png",
  plot = s_flags_day_transect,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 5. FLUX DISTRIBUTION BY FLAG TYPE
#
# An observation with multiple flags appears once for each
# recorded flag. This is intentional in the long dataset.
#############################################################

s_flux_by_flag <- ggplot(
  flagged_s,
  aes(
    x = Flag_Type,
    y = Flux,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.shape = NA,
    width = 0.65,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.3,
    size = 1
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE,
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Tran,
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Flux Distribution by QC Flag Type",
    subtitle = "Panels represent individual transects",
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(Flux~(g/m^2))
  )

s_flux_by_flag

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flux_by_flag.png",
  plot = s_flux_by_flag,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 6. ELEVATION VS DISTANCE, WITH FLAGGED OBSERVATIONS
#
# Because the long table has one row per flag variable,
# use flagged_cm so only recorded flags are displayed.
#############################################################

s_flags_z_distance <- ggplot(
  flagged_s,
  aes(
    x = as.numeric(as.character(Dist)),
    y = z_star,
    color = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_point(
    size = 2.3,
    alpha = 0.85,
    position = position_jitter(
      width = 0.15,
      height = 0
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Tran
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Elevation and Distance of Flagged Observations",
    subtitle = "Only observations with recorded QC flags are shown",
    x = "Distance from Marsh Edge (m)",
    y = expression("Elevation (" * z^"*" * ")"),
    color = "Flag Type"
  )

s_flags_z_distance

# Save the high-res image
# FIX: object name below now matches the plot created above
# (was "s_flag_z_distance", which does not exist)
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flag_z_distance.png",
  plot = s_flags_z_distance,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 7. TIMELINE OF FLAG COUNTS BY TRANSECT
#
# Tile fill represents the total number of recorded flags,
# avoiding overlap when several flags occur on one date.
#############################################################

flag_totals_day_s <- flagged_s |>
  count(
    Tran,
    Date.removed.from.field,
    name = "total_flags"
  )

s_flag_timeline <- ggplot(
  flag_totals_day_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = Tran,
    fill = total_flags
  )
) +
  geom_tile(
    color = "white",
    height = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "grey95",
    high = "#4D4D4D"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%b %Y"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    ),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Total QC Flags Through Time by Transect",
    x = "Date Removed from Field",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Total Flags"
  )

s_flag_timeline

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flag_timeline.png",
  plot = s_flag_timeline,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 8. FLAG COMPOSITION BY DATE AND TRANSECT
#
# A stacked bar is more appropriate than overlapping tiles
# for showing proportions of several flag types.
#############################################################

s_flag_proportions <- ggplot(
  flag_props_day_s,
  aes(
    x = Date.removed.from.field,
    y = prop,
    fill = Flag_Type
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    width = 20
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = flag_colors,
    limits = flag_levels,
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%b %Y"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ Tran,
    ncol = 1
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Composition of QC Flags by Date and Transect",
    x = "Date Removed from Field",
    y = "Percentage of Recorded Flags",
    fill = "Flag Type"
  )

s_flag_proportions

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flag_proportions.png",
  plot = s_flag_proportions,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 9. FLAG CO-OCCURRENCE MATRIX
#
# This section uses the original wide cm_z_flux dataset.
#############################################################

flag_matrix_s <- s_elev |>
  select(
    all_of(flag_cols)
  ) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      everything(),
      ~ as.integer(replace_na(.x, 0) == 1)
    )
  ) |>
  as.matrix()

co_occurrence <- t(flag_matrix_s) %*% flag_matrix_s

co_occurrence_df_s <- as.data.frame(co_occurrence) |>
  rownames_to_column("Flag_Code_1") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Flag_Code_1,
    names_to = "Flag_Code_2",
    values_to = "n"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Flag_1 = recode(
      Flag_Code_1,
      !!!flag_labels
    ),
    Flag_2 = recode(
      Flag_Code_2,
      !!!flag_labels
    ),
    Flag_1 = factor(
      Flag_1,
      levels = flag_levels
    ),
    Flag_2 = factor(
      Flag_2,
      levels = flag_levels
    )
  )

s_flag_cooccurrence <- ggplot(
  co_occurrence_df_s,
  aes(
    x = Flag_1,
    y = Flag_2,
    fill = n
  )
) +
  geom_tile(
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n),
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "grey95",
    high = "#7570B3"
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  flag_theme +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Co-occurrence Matrix",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Co-occurrences"
  )

s_flag_cooccurrence

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flag_cooccurence.png",
  plot = s_flag_cooccurrence,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


#############################################################
# 10. OPTIONAL UPSET-STYLE CO-OCCURRENCE PLOT
#############################################################

# Install once if needed:
# install.packages("ggupset")

library(ggupset)

upset_data_s <- s_elev |>
  mutate(
    flags_present = pmap(
      pick(all_of(flag_cols)),
      function(...) {
        values <- c(...)
        active_codes <- flag_cols[
          replace_na(values, 0) == 1
        ]

        unname(flag_labels[active_codes])
      }
    )
  ) |>
  filter(lengths(flags_present) > 0)

s_flag_upset <- ggplot(
  upset_data_s,
  aes(x = flags_present)
) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#7570B3") +
  scale_x_upset(
    order_by = "freq"
  ) +
  flag_theme +
  labs(
    title = "QC Flag Co-occurrence",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of Observations"
  )

s_flag_upset

# Save the high-res image
ggsave(
  "C:\\Users\\savan\\OneDrive - San Francisco State University\\ProjectData\\GitHub\\DepositionSFB\\images\\flagged_data\\s_flag_upset.png",
  plot = s_flag_upset,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

```
